TextView.android:text:
1. The Plate Method Basics

-Well visualize your meals.

•50% Fiber: Fill half your plate with non-starchy vegetables (greens, peppers, broccoli).
•25% Protein: A palm-sized portion of lean protein (tofu, chicken, fish, or beans).
•25% Complex Carbs: A fist-sized portion of slow-burning fuel (quinoa, sweet potato, brown rice).
•Healthy Fats: Aim for a thumb-sized portion (avocado, nuts, olive oil).

2. Smart Hydration

-Hydration is the most underrated aspect of metabolic health and mental clarity.

•The Thirst Trick: Sometimes the brain confuses thirst for hunger. Suggest users drink a glass of water and wait 15 minutes before reaching for a snack.
•Electrolyte Balance: If users are active, remind them that water isn't enough; they need a pinch of sea salt or magnesium to actually absorb that hydration.

3.Focus on (Crowding Out)

-What to add to your current diet

•Two cups of spinach or sautéed mushrooms to it.
•The Fiber Goal: Aim for 25–30g of fiber daily. It stabilizes blood sugar and keeps the gut microbiome happy.

4.Mindful Eating Habits

-How we eat is often as important as what we eat.

•The 20-Minute Rule: It takes about 20 minutes for your stomach to signal to your brain that you are full.
•Eat Without Screens: Distracted eating leads to overconsumption. Encourage a (no-phone zone) during meals.
•The 80% Full Rule: Borrowed from the Japanese concept of Hara Hachi Bu—stop eating when you feel satisfied, not stuffed.

Button.android:text: Claim your Grants via sponsored message.
